{{Quote|This place sure has changed a lot since I was last here. Now they have a nicer world to enslave.|Leia Organa|<ref name="SW:GB" />}}
Formerly the desolate [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] known as '''Geddes''', the [[Terraforming/Legends|terraformed]] world of '''Hanoon''' was home to several strategic [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] outposts designed to increase Imperial presence in that [[Star system/Legends|system]].

==Description==
Geddes was one of many [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] held loosely in [[Krant]]'s [[Gravity well/Legends|gravity well]].<ref name="SW:GB DataBank">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' DataBank</ref> Krant was a [[Planet/Legends|world]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Krant system/Legends|Krant system]] at [[Galactic coordinates/Legends|galactic coordinates]] R-14.<ref name="EA" /> After the [[Terraforming/Legends|terraforming]] process had been completed, the asteroid had canals and snow-covered rocks. Due to the large amount a raw materials on the asteroid, a number of mining operations had been set up even before the Empire took over.<ref name="SW:GB" />

==History==
Around [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the events of the mission between [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]] and [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]].</ref> Geddes became the site of an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] research facility. After the Imperials located the [[Vor'Na'Tu]], an ancient and powerful [[Jedi artifact/Legends|Jedi artifact]], on Krant, it was shipped to Geddes' [[Sith temple/Legends|Sith Temple]] to await retrieval by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. After the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] discovered the location of the Vor'Na'Tu, a strike force led by [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and the exiled [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Echuu Shen-Jon]], the former keeper of the Vor'Na'Tu, was sent to Geddes aboard a [[Unidentified GR-75 medium transport (Krant)|transport]]. They were joined by a [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Army/Legends|army]]. The strike force struck at the research facility and destroyed the temple, freeing the Vor'Na'Tu. The force retreated back to the transport, but was ambushed by Darth Vader. Shen-Jon shattered the artifact and sacrificed himself by [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|fighting]] Vader to allow the others to escape.<ref name="SW:GB" />

[[File:Yittreas_fortresses_destroyed.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The aftermath of the planetoid's liberation.]]
After the [[Krantian Civil War]], [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Yittreas]] escaped to Geddes and fortified the asteroid.<ref name="SW:GB DataBank" /> By [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]],<ref name="EA Time">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the Battle of Hanoon in [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]].</ref> Geddes had been terraformed and renamed Hanoon, and the [[City/Legends|cities]] of [[Libreck]], [[Magentic]], [[Reeche]], and [[Reticul]] had been established, with [[New Geddes]] as the [[Capital/Legends|capital]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Organa and her brother, the Jedi [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], returned to Hanoon after a visitation from Shen-Jon's [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]]. Aided by the [[New Republic Army]] and the [[Hanoon Imperial Resistance]], Skywalker recovered the shards of the Vor'Na'Tu and placed them in a newly-built Jedi Temple. Organa and Skywalker then decided to [[Battle of Hanoon|liberate]] the rest of the planetoid, and marched on New Geddes to free the [[Terraform Processor]]. The Republic forces succeeded in freeing the Terraform Processor, and destroyed Moff Yittreas' [[Fortress/Legends|Fortress]], killing the Moff. During the battle, Imperial forces tried to break into the Temple holding the Vor'Na'Tu's remains, forcing Skywalker to shatter the artifact into dust, thereby ending its threat. With the Imperials defeated, Hanoon joined the New Republic.<ref name="SW:GB" />

==Behind the scenes==
Geddes/Hanoon was first mentioned in the [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] for the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''. The asteroid appeared throughout the Princess Leia campaign and received its own entry in the game's DataBank. The campaign was later included in the game's [[2002]] expansion pack, [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns|''Clone Campaigns'']]. [[2009]]'s reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' provided dates for the battles which took place on Hanoon.
